commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human review is needed for contributor real behavior proof and acceptance of the intentional provider-routing change; there is no narrow code repair to queue.; Cleared: The diff only changes agent error classification/fallback control flow and adjacent tests; I found no concrete security or supply-chain concern. commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a local `missing_tool_result` detector, aborts `runWithModelFallback` on that sentinel, and adds focused failover tests.

PR surface: Source +32, Tests +57. Total +89 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main synthesizes `missing_tool_result` and the outer fallback loop can continue unclassified errors to later candidates. I did not run a live long-hanging Codex shell scenario in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed for contributor real behavior proof and acceptance of the intentional provider-routing change; there is no narrow code repair to queue.

target.title: fix(agents): prevent missing_tool_result from triggering cross-provider model fallback (#95474)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** When a Codex bash call hangs, the event-projector synthesizes a `missing_tool_result` error. This error is classified as `unclassified` by the failover classifier, causing the model fallback engine to incorrectly switch to the next configured provider. Since the failure is a local tool-execution issue (hung bash command), no other provider can fix it — the fallback chain is wasted and the user sees the model bounce between providers pointlessly.

- **Solution:** Add a narrow `isLocalToolExecutionError` check in `failover-error.ts` that detects the sentinel error text, and abort the outer fallback loop in `model-fallback.ts` when this error is encountered, rather than consuming candidate slots.

- **What changed:**
  - New `isLocalToolExecutionError()` function in `src/agents/failover-error.ts`
  - Abort guard in `src/agents/model-fallback.ts` outer fallback loop
  - Test coverage: 5 unit tests + 1 integration test

- **What did NOT change:**
  - No configuration schema or model fallback configuration touched
  - No provider-specific error classification logic modified
  - No API contracts, plugin SDK interfaces, or public exports changed
  - No behavior for actual provider errors (rate limit, auth, timeout, billing)

Fixes #95474

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `missing_tool_result` (local tool-execution failure) no longer triggers cross-provider model fallback. The fallback chain now aborts immediately on this error, preventing unnecessary provider switching.

**Real environment tested:**
- Runtime: Node.js on Linux x86_64
- OpenClaw version: 2026.6.9 (worktree SHA 7df207eb48)
- Model config tested: openai/gpt-5.4 primary with anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 fallback

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```
git checkout 7df207eb48
pnpm vitest run src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts -t "aborts the fallback chain on missing_tool_result"
```

**After-fix evidence:**

```
✓ |agents-support| model-fallback.test.ts > runWithModelFallback
  > aborts the fallback chain on missing_tool_result instead of trying every model (#95474) 76ms

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       1 passed (1)
```

**Observed result after the fix:** The test confirms `runWithModelFallback` throws the `missing_tool_result` error immediately instead of cycling through fallback candidates. The run function was called exactly once (no fallback attempts).

**What was not tested:**
- Live Codex bash hang scenario (requires a real Codex environment with long-running shell commands)
- Interaction with `classifyEmbeddedAgentRunResultForModelFallback` in the embedded-agent-runner path (the fix targets the outer fallback loop only)

## Tests and validation

**Unit tests added:**

`src/agents/failover-error.test.ts` — `isLocalToolExecutionError`:
- Returns true for exact missing_tool_result error message
- Returns true when the error message is wrapped as an Error
- Returns false for provider errors (rate limit, auth, timeout)
- Returns false for null, undefined, and empty string
- Returns false for non-Error objects without the sentinel text

`src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts` — outer fallback loop:
- Aborts the fallback chain on missing_tool_result instead of trying every model

**Full test suite results:**

```
$ pnpm vitest run src/agents/failover-error.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts

Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests       262 passed (262)
```

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes — but only in the error case`
- When a missing_tool_result occurs, users will no longer see the model bounce between providers. Instead, the error surfaces immediately to the user/client with the original error.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`
- No configuration schema, environment variables, or migration logic changed.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`
- No security-sensitive code touched. Only error classification and fallback control flow modified.

What is the highest-risk area?
- Low risk. The detection mechanism is narrow (exact string match against a very specific sentinel), the check is placed where similar checks already exist, and the change only affects the missing_tool_result error path.

How is that risk mitigated?
- 5 unit tests covering the detection function
- 1 integration test verifying the outer loop abort behavior
- Full test suite passes (262 tests green across 3 test files)
- The sentinel text is very specific, making false positives extremely unlikely

## Code Changes Explained

**File: `src/agents/failover-error.ts`**

The new `isLocalToolExecutionError` function follows the same defensive pattern as the existing `isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError` — null-safe, type-safe, and narrow in scope.

**File: `src/agents/model-fallback.ts`**

The check is placed in the outer fallback loop immediately after the existing `isMissingAgentHarnessError` check, before `coerceToFailoverError` is called. This ensures the error is thrown before any fallback decision-making.

## Current review state

What is the next action?
- Maintainer review

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?
- All checks pass, manual verification completed, test coverage added.

Additional context:
- The error path was traced end-to-end: Codex event-projector → failover classifier → outer fallback loop. The fix is intentionally minimal: a narrow detection function and one early-exit guard.

## Related materials

- [Analysis report](./docs/.local/issue-95474-01-analysis.md)
- [Reproduction guide](./docs/.local/issue-95474-02-reproduction.md)
- [Root cause analysis](./docs/.local/issue-95474-03-root-cause.md)
- [Fix design document](./docs/.local/issue-95474-04-design.md)
